entities.summary: Continuous batching scheduler with preemption for KV cache management

detail.name: Scheduler
detail.description: FCFS request scheduler managing waiting and running deques. Coordinates with BlockManager for allocation.
detail.why: Multiple requests compete for limited GPU memory — need policy to decide who runs

takeaway.explain: Add new requests to running batch mid-generation. No waiting for entire batch to finish. Maximizes GPU utilization.
takeaway.rationale: Used here because: Multiple requests compete for limited GPU memory — need policy to decide who runs
takeaway.cross_lang: No direct equivalent — LLM serving specific. Conceptually similar to OS process scheduling
takeaway.gotcha: Preemption priority must be carefully tuned — aggressive preemption wastes computed tokens

takeaway.explain: Forcibly pause running task to free resources for others. Preempted task re-queued. Prevents starvation.
takeaway.rationale: Used here because: Multiple requests compete for limited GPU memory — need policy to decide who runs

detail.design: Two deques rather than priority queue — preemption needs O(1) removal of specific sequence
detail.convention: All state mutations inside schedule() — external code only calls add() and reads results
detail.perf: Called every step; O(waiting + running) per call

entities.summary: Initialize waiting and running deques for FCFS scheduling

detail.name: __init__
detail.description: Two deques: waiting (submitted, not yet scheduled) and running (actively generating).

takeaway.explain: O(1) append/pop from both ends. Perfect for FCFS (append right, pop left) with preemption (append left for priority re-queue).
takeaway.rationale: Two deques: waiting (submitted, not yet scheduled) and running (actively generating)

entities.summary: Check if all requests are done

detail.name: is_finished
detail.description: True when both waiting and running deques are empty.

takeaway.explain: System is done when all queues are empty. Simple but must check ALL queues — easy to miss one.
takeaway.rationale: True when both waiting and running deques are empty

entities.summary: Enqueue new request to waiting deque

detail.name: add
detail.description: Adds sequence to back of waiting queue. Will be scheduled when resources available.

takeaway.explain: Append to queue tail. Processing order determined by queue discipline (here: FCFS). Decouples arrival from execution.
takeaway.rationale: Adds sequence to back of waiting queue

entities.summary: Core scheduling loop: allocate or preempt based on block availability

detail.name: schedule
detail.description: Iterates waiting sequences, attempts allocation. If insufficient blocks, preempts from running set.
detail.why: Must decide which sequences advance this step by checking block availability

takeaway.explain: Process requests front-to-back, allocate greedily. Simple but may not be globally optimal. Good enough for latency-sensitive inference.
takeaway.rationale: Used here because: Must decide which sequences advance this step by checking block availability
takeaway.cross_lang: OS CPU scheduler (CFS), Go goroutine scheduler, Java ForkJoinPool work stealing
takeaway.gotcha: Greedy can starve long-running requests — need fairness bounds or priority aging

detail.design: Processes waiting front-to-back for fairness; separates prefill from decode

entities.summary: Evict a running sequence to free its KV cache blocks

detail.name: preempt
detail.description: Moves sequence from running back to waiting, deallocates its blocks.

takeaway.explain: Free resources from lower-priority work to serve higher-priority or new work. The running sequence loses its KV cache and must recompute.
takeaway.rationale: Moves sequence from running back to waiting, deallocates its blocks

entities.summary: Finalize step: remove finished sequences, update block hashes

detail.name: postprocess
detail.description: Cleans up completed sequences, deallocates their blocks, updates prefix cache.

takeaway.explain: After main logic, sweep to clean up completed items. Separate from main loop for clarity. Common pattern in game loops and engines.
takeaway.rationale: Cleans up completed sequences, deallocates their blocks, updates prefix cache
